page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance features 4 dedicated physical cores with single-thread execution per core, meaning hyperthreading is absent. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance in several key areas, alongside some architectural limitations. The instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng single-core (1519.59) and multi-core (6052.47) CPU tests, as well as in Passmark database operations (2599.67). Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks also fall into the average tier, with read speeds of 71.58 GB/sec and write speeds of 34.94 GB/sec. However, performance is weaker in other areas; Geekbench single-core (957.0) and multi-core (3208.0) scores are in the bottom 25%, while Passmark CPU score (2690.0) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (7.34 GB/sec) are rated as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6gd.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU density combined with local NVMe storage, making it a viable option for workloads requiring local scratch space and high memory capacity without the premium of x86 licensing. However, the trade-off lies in its lower raw computational throughput and weaker single-threaded performance compared to newer processor generations. This hardware profile is applicable for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations that benefit from local SSD storage, rather than compute-heavy tasks or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 1 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and Passmark database operations, though it exhibits weaker results in Geekbench and single-threaded decompression tasks. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications requiring local scratch space, making it applicable for database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads that do not demand high single-core compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, offering average database performance and a high memory-to-core ratio.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 Graviton vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 physical cores on AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 architecture
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Average performance in stress-ng CPU and Passmark database benchmarks
Applicable for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
